step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows a real-time recommendation system at Peloton. 1. The Peloton client sends a recommendation request to the EKS Hydration Service. 2. The Hydration Service passes this to the EKS Recommendation Service (the orchestrator). 3. The Recommendation Service retrieves candidates from the Graph Database (Neptune), features from the DynamoDB Feature Store, and model info from the Model Registry. 4. These are sent to the EKS Inference Service to score classes. 5. Results go back through the Recommendation Service and Hydration Service to the Client. 6. Simultaneously, the Recommendation Service logs events via MSK, which flow back to S3. 7. Offline, EMR pulls S3 data to preprocess features (populating Neptune and DynamoDB) and feeds the training data to EKS Model Training, which outputs to the Model Registry.

graph.name: Peloton Real-time Personalization and Recommendation Engine
graph.notes: Hybrid real-time ML inference and daily offline ML training architecture built using EKS, EMR, and various managed storage systems.

nodes.name: Client
nodes.notes: Peloton Member Interface (bike, tread, or mobile app).

nodes.name: Amazon EKS Hydration Service
nodes.notes: Hydrates generated recommendations for delivery to clients.

nodes.name: Amazon EKS Recommendation Service
nodes.notes: Orchestrates candidate generation, feature retrieval, and inference requests.

nodes.name: Amazon EKS Inference Service
nodes.notes: Scores candidate workouts using trained machine learning models.

nodes.name: Graph Database
nodes.notes: Maintains social graph, workout, and instructor connections for candidate generation.

nodes.name: Amazon DynamoDB Feature Store
nodes.notes: Low-latency store for user feature vectors used during model scoring.

nodes.name: Model Registry
nodes.notes: Stores trained ML model versions and artifacts.

nodes.name: Amazon MSK Event Logging
nodes.notes: Logs recommendation events asynchronously for training and analytics.

nodes.name: Amazon S3 Historical Data
nodes.notes: Datalake storing historical workout logs and feedback loop event data.

nodes.name: Amazon EMR Data Preprocessing
nodes.notes: Runs daily Spark jobs to prepare training features and populate low-latency databases.

nodes.name: Amazon EKS Model Training
nodes.notes: GPU-accelerated daily batch training of machine learning models.

edges.notes: Streams feedback events to historical storage
